function_explanation: Computes the power-law power spectral density (PSD) for gravitational wave background signals, commonly used in pulsar timing array analysis.
function_docstring:
Compute power-law power spectral density S(f) for gravitational wave signals.

Calculates the power spectral density using the formula:
S(f) = A^2 / (12 * pi^2) * (f / f_yr)^(-gamma) * f_yr^(-3)

where A = 10^log10_A and f_yr = 1/(365.25 * 86400) Hz is the reference
frequency corresponding to one year.

Args:
    freqs: Array of frequencies at which to evaluate the PSD, in Hz.
        Can be a scalar or array of any shape.
    log10_A: Base-10 logarithm of the characteristic strain amplitude A.
        Typically in the range of -18 to -12 for gravitational wave backgrounds.
    gamma: Spectral index (power-law exponent) of the PSD. A value of
        gamma=13/3 corresponds to a gravitational wave background from
        supermassive black hole binaries.

Returns:
    Power spectral density values with the same shape as the input freqs
    array, in units of s^3 (or 1/Hz^3).
usage_example:
import numpy as np
from agent_powerlaw_psd import powerlaw_psd

# Define frequency array (e.g., from 1 nHz to 100 nHz)
freqs = np.logspace(-9, -7, 100)  # 100 frequencies from 1 nHz to 100 nHz

# Typical parameters for a stochastic gravitational wave background
log10_A = -15.0  # Characteristic strain amplitude
gamma = 13.0 / 3.0  # Spectral index for SMBHB background

# Compute the power spectral density
psd = powerlaw_psd(freqs, log10_A, gamma)

print(f"PSD at lowest frequency: {psd[0]:.2e}")
print(f"PSD at highest frequency: {psd[-1]:.2e}")
